{{Shinto shrine | Name = Konda Hachimangu | Image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | Annual Festival = September 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Konda Hachimangu''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. In the Heian period, the shrine was moved about one chō (approximately 109 meters) south to its current location in 1051 during the reign of Emperor Go-Reizei. This event marked the beginning of imperial patronage, with later renovations supported by prominent figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 and {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}}.[2] Throughout history, the shrine received patronage from warrior clans such as the Minamoto, but it suffered destruction during internal conflicts in the late Muromachi period. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == ### National Treasures - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. ### Important Cultural Properties - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). ### Natural Monuments - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:last 100]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. In the Heian period, the shrine was moved about one chō (approximately 109 meters) south to its current location in 1051 during the reign of Emperor Go-Reizei. This event marked the beginning of imperial patronage, with later renovations supported by prominent figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 and {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}}.[2] Throughout history, the shrine received patronage from warrior clans such as the Minamoto, but it suffered destruction during internal conflicts in the late Muromachi period. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - **Main Shrine (Honden):** Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - **Central Gate (Chūmon):** Constructed in 1606. - **Haiden (Worship Hall):** Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - **Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):** Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - **Hōjō Bridge:** A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - **Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):** A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - **Other structures:** - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == ### Hinomaru Sacred Sake During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == ### National Treasures - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. ### Important Cultural Properties - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). ### Natural Monuments - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Konda Hachimangu | Image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | Annual Festival = September 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Konda Hachimangu''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. In the Heian period, the shrine was moved about one chō (approximately 109 meters) south to its current location in 1051 during the reign of Emperor Go-Reizei. This event marked the beginning of imperial patronage, with later renovations supported by prominent figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 and {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}}.[2] Throughout history, the shrine received patronage from warrior clans such as the Minamoto, but it suffered destruction during internal conflicts in the late Muromachi period. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == ### Hinomaru Sacred Sake During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == ### National Treasures - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. ### Important Cultural Properties - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). ### Natural Monuments - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == ### Hinomaru Sacred Sake During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == ### National Treasures - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. ### Important Cultural Properties - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). ### Natural Monuments - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. In the Heian period, the shrine was moved about one chō (approximately 109 meters) south to its current location in 1051 during the reign of Emperor Go-Reizei. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. In the Heian period, the shrine was moved about one chō (approximately 109 meters) south to its current location in 1051 during the reign of Emperor Go-Reizei. This event marked the beginning of imperial patronage, with later renovations supported by prominent figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 and {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}}.[2] Throughout history, the shrine received patronage from warrior clans such as the Minamoto, but it suffered destruction during internal conflicts in the late Muromachi period. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Konda Hachimangu''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. In the Heian period, the shrine was moved about one chō (approximately 109 meters) south to its current location in 1051 during the reign of Emperor Go-Reizei. This event marked the beginning of imperial patronage, with later renovations supported by prominent figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 and {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}}.[2] Throughout history, the shrine received patronage from warrior clans such as the Minamoto, but it suffered destruction during internal conflicts in the late Muromachi period. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Konda Hachimangu''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. In the Heian period, the shrine was moved about one chō (approximately 109 meters) south to its current location in 1051 during the reign of Emperor Go-Reizei. This event marked the beginning of imperial patronage, with later renovations supported by prominent figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 and {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}}.[2] Throughout history, the shrine received patronage from warrior clans such as the Minamoto, but it suffered destruction during internal conflicts in the late Muromachi period. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] This event marked the beginning of imperial patronage, with later renovations supported by prominent figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 and {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}}.[2] Throughout history, the shrine received patronage from warrior clans such as the Minamoto, but it suffered destruction during internal conflicts in the late Muromachi period. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Konda Hachimangu''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} and is situated adjacent to the southern side of {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the tomb of Emperor Ōjin. == History == According to shrine tradition, Konda Hachimangu was established in the 20th year of Emperor Kinmei's reign (559) as a mausoleum shrine in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb, aimed at restoring {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}} (a Japanese outpost in Korea). It claims to be the oldest Hachimangu shrine.[1] The site is closely connected to Emperor Ōjin, who is believed to have lived here during his childhood, and to his mother, Empress Nakatsuhime, who was the daughter of Prince Konda no Mawakao, a local ruler. During the Nara period, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established a temple, Naganozan Gokokuji, within the shrine grounds as its {{ill|Jingū-ji|ja|神宮寺}}. In the Heian period, the shrine was moved about one chō (approximately 109 meters) south to its current location in 1051 during the reign of Emperor Go-Reizei. This event marked the beginning of imperial patronage, with later renovations supported by prominent figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 and {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}}.[2] Throughout history, the shrine received patronage from warrior clans such as the Minamoto, but it suffered destruction during internal conflicts in the late Muromachi period. After being confiscated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine was later rebuilt by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. Subsequent renovations were carried out by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} and supported by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}.[3] Following the {{ill|Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the Meiji period, the temple structures within the shrine grounds were removed. == Deities == - Principal deities: - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Southern Gate}}]] - '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1606 under Toyotomi Hideyori’s patronage. - '''Central Gate (Chūmon):''' Constructed in 1606. - '''Haiden (Worship Hall):''' Completed in 1606 but left partially unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan.[4] - '''Tōsō Shrine (Masamune-sha):''' Enshrines Susanoo-no-Mikoto. Originally located about 100 meters east, it was moved to the main grounds in 1907.[5] - '''Hōjō Bridge:''' A designated cultural property of Habikino City, it once connected the shrine to the rear mound of Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. - '''Stone Pagoda (Tahōtō):''' A rare stone-built three-story pagoda. - '''Other structures:''' - South Gate (remnant of Naganozan Gokokuji). - Treasure Hall (Honden Hōden) and other auxiliary buildings. == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Sake === During the New Year’s festival, a sacred sake made from local red wine is distributed free of charge. Its red color contrasts with the white cup, earning it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Sake" after the Japanese flag.[6] == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === - {{ill|Chiriji Raden Kondō-sō Mikoshi|ja|塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿}} (Portable shrine, Kamakura period). - Gilded and openwork saddle fittings from Konda Gobyoyama Kofun. === Important Cultural Properties === - Painted scrolls depicting Empress Jingū’s legends (Muromachi period). - Gilded saddle inlay with mother-of-pearl (Kamakura period). === Natural Monuments === - Large hackberry tree (designated by Osaka Prefecture). == Access == - Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}} {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangu] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-ranked shrines)|ja|府社}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachiman Shrine''' (誉田八幡宮, Konda Hachiman-gū) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Habikino**, **Osaka Prefecture**, Japan. The shrine's **historical rank** is that of a **former fusha (府社)**. It is situated south of the **Konda Gobyōyama Kofun**, the **Imperial Mausoleum of Emperor Ōjin**. == History == According to the shrine legend, **Emperor Kinmei**, aiming to restore the ancient **Mimana state (任那)**, established the shrine in the 20th year of his reign (559 CE). It is believed to be the **oldest Hachiman Shrine**.[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/bukkaku/2449.html Konda Hachiman Shrine – Habikino City], Retrieved March 17, 2019. The shrine holds special significance for Emperor Ōjin as: 1. It was built near his **mausoleum**, the **Konda Gobyōyama Kofun**. 2. It is located on the site where **Emperor Ōjin** is believed to have lived during his childhood. 3. His **mother, Princess Nakatsuhime (仲津姫)**, was the daughter of **Prince Konda no Masawaka** and governed the area. During the **Nara period**, **Gyōki (行基)** founded **Naganoyama Gokoku Temple (長野山護国寺)** within the shrine grounds as a **Shingonji temple**. In **Eishō 6 (1051)**, the shrine was moved about one town south to its present location. During this time, **Emperor Go-Reizei** visited the shrine. Since **Hachiman-no-kami** became the patron deity of the **Minamoto clan**, the shrine flourished with their support. In **Kenkū 7 (1196)**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** restored the shrine and donated **national treasures** such as portable shrines (mikoshi). Additionally, **Ashikaga Yoshinori**, the sixth shogun of the **Muromachi Shogunate**, offered **the Konda Ancestral Shrine Chronicle (誉田宗廟縁起)** and **the Empress Jingū Chronicle (神功皇后縁起)** as gifts. During the **Nanbokuchō period** and **Sengoku period**, the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō clan acted as the shrine's caretakers. However, the **Hatakeyama clan's conflicts** during the **Kyōtoku Rebellion (享徳の乱, 1454)** led to the destruction of the main hall and temple buildings. Later, **Oda Nobunaga** confiscated all the shrine's landholdings. The shrine was rebuilt under **Toyotomi Hideyoshi**, who granted **200 koku** of land. However, the shrine suffered another fire in **Tenshō 14 (1586)**. Eventually, the shrine's landholdings were relocated to **Komuro Village**, **Shiki District**, Kawachi Province. In **Keichō 11 (1606)**, **Toyotomi Hideyori** ordered **Katagiri Katsumoto** to supervise the reconstruction of the shrine’s structures. However, due to the **Siege of Osaka** and the fall of the **Toyotomi clan**, the interior remained unfinished. The **Tokugawa Shogunate** maintained the **200 koku grant** and funded subsequent restorations. The temple, **Naganoyama Gokokuji**, once had **fifteen sub-temples**, while the shrine had **thirteen priestly houses**, but during the **Meiji period**, the buildings were demolished during **Shinto-Buddhist separation** and **Haibutsu Kishaku (abolition of Buddhism)**. Only the **South Gate** survived. During this period, **Momoi Naomasa**, a famous **Bakumatsu swordsman**, served as the chief priest. == Deities == The main deities of the shrine are: * **Emperor Ōjin (応神天皇)** * **Empress Jingū (神功皇后)** * **Emperor Chūai (仲哀天皇)** == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate (Minamimon)}}]] * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Rebuilt in **Keichō 11 (1606)** under **Toyotomi Hideyori**. * **Central Gate (Chūmon)** – Built under Toyotomi’s supervision. * **Worship Hall (Haiden)** – Also rebuilt in **Keichō 11 (1606)** and later renovated in the **Kan'ei era (1624-1645)**. * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** – Former **Shikinaisha**, dedicated to **Susanoo-no-Mikoto (素盞鳴尊)**. Originally located 100 meters east of the shrine but moved to the current grounds in **1907**.[http://www.buccyake-kojiki.com/archives/1063947330.html Masamune Shrine - Habikino City] * **Hōjō Bridge** – Constructed in the **late 17th to early 18th century**, designated a **Habikino City Cultural Property**. * **Stone Pagoda (Sōtō)** – A **three-storied stone pagoda**. * Various smaller shrines and structures, including: - **Inari Shrine (姫待稲荷社)** - **Ebisu Shrine (恵比寿社)** - **Emadō (絵馬堂)** - **South Gate** - **Treasure Hall** - **Sacred Pond (放生池, Hōjō-ike)** == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * **Chirijiraden Kondō Portable Shrine** (鎌倉時代) * **Gold-Copper Ornamented Saddle**, excavated from the **Maruyama Kofun**, an auxiliary tomb of Emperor Ōjin === Important Cultural Properties === * **Illustrated Chronicles of Empress Jingū (紙本著色神功皇后縁起)** * **Eleven Bugaku Masks** (鎌倉 - Muromachi periods) === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Sword with Signature by Takai Nobuyoshi (太刀 銘高井信吉)** === Natural Monuments === * **Hackberry Tree at Konda Hachiman Shrine** == Festivals == One notable custom is the **Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒)** served during the **New Year's Ceremony (歳旦祭)**. The wine is a **red wine** produced in **Habikino**, a major grape-growing region of Osaka. The contrast of the red wine with a **white sake cup** earned it the name **Hinomaru Sacred Wine**, referencing the **Japanese flag**.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2019/01/02/hinomarumiki/ Report on Hinomaru Sacred Wine – Minakawa] == Transportation == * **Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line**, **Furuichi Station**, 10-minute walk == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official website of Konda Hachiman Shrine] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/ Official Osaka Shrine Bureau page for Konda Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Fusha Ranked Shrines]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Historical Artifacts)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Temples and Shrines Dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]][[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-ranked shrines)|ja|府社}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The shrine's former rank under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Konda Goryoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Princess Nakatsu|ja|仲津姫}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in 1196 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō 11), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middle gate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō 11) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** - South Gate - Shrine Storehouse - Sacred Object Storehouse - Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|New Year’s Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun
