Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|12=draft}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} | rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-ranked shrines)|ja|府社|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|12=draft}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's former rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|12=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|12=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|12=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|12=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|12=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|12=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|12=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|12=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|12=draft}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} | rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-ranked shrines)|ja|府社|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|12=draft}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's former rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|12=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|12=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|12=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|12=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|12=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|12=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|12=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|12=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft Kinmei]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|12=draft}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|12=draft}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|12=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|12=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|12=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|12=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|12=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|12=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|12=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|12=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|12=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|12=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|12=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|12=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|12=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|12=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|12=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|12=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Temples and Shrines Dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|12=draft}}. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|12=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|12=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|12=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|12=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|12=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|12=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|12=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 誉田八幡宮 |画像 = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]誉田3丁目2-8 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神 = [[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、[[仲哀天皇]] |社格 = 旧[[府社]] |創建 = [[欽明天皇]]20年（[[559年]]） |本殿= |別名= |札所等 = [[河内飛鳥古社寺霊場]]客番 |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事= }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''誉田八幡宮'''（こんだはちまんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]誉田にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。[[誉田御廟山古墳]]（応神天皇陵）の南に隣接して鎮座する。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[欽明天皇]]20年（[[559年]]）に[[任那]]の復興を目指した欽明天皇によって、[[応神天皇]]陵前に神廟が設置されたことをもって創建としており、最古の[[八幡宮]]を称している[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunkazaihogoka/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/bukkaku/2449.html 誉田八幡宮 - 羽曳野市]2019年3月17日　閲覧。なお、この地は応神天皇陵（誉田御廟山古墳）がある地であるが、応神天皇が幼少の頃に居住していたところでもあり、また、[[皇后]]の[[仲姫命|仲津姫]]はこの地を治める誉田真若王の娘でもあり、応神天皇にとって特別なゆかりのある地である。 [[奈良時代]]には[[行基]]によって[[神宮寺]]である長野山護国寺が境内に創建されている。 [[平安時代]]の[[永承]]6年（[[1051年]]）には創建の地より1町ほど南にある現在地に遷座し、[[後冷泉天皇]]による行幸が行われた。 八幡神が[[源氏]]の[[氏神]]とされることから、[[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]）には[[源頼朝]]によって社殿が修復され[[国宝]]の神輿などが寄進されている。[[室町幕府]]第6代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]][[足利義教]]は、[[重要文化財]]の｢誉田宗廟縁起｣と｢[[神功皇后]]縁起｣を奉納している。 このように、当社は源姓を名乗る歴代の将軍をはじめ、武家の信仰を受けて栄えた。 [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]にかけては、別当職の誉田三河入道一族によって保護されたが、[[享徳]]3年（[[1454年]]）より始まった[[河内国|河内]]守護・[[畠山氏]]の内紛により社殿・伽藍を焼失し荒廃した。[[三好氏]]により滅ぼされた応神天皇末裔の誉田氏の一部は、[[四国]]や[[九州]]に逃れ、また、[[江戸時代]]には、関西一円の[[香具師]]（やし）（[[露天商]]）の元締めとして君臨したといわれている。 河内国を支配下に置いた[[織田信長]]により社領は全て没収された。その後、[[豊臣秀吉]]により社領200石が寄進されて社殿が再建されたが、[[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）に焼失してしまった。後に社領200石は河内国[[志紀郡]]古室村に転換されている。 [[慶長]]11年（[[1606年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]が[[片桐且元]]を普請奉行に任命して本殿・中門・拝殿などの再建を行っている。しかし、拝殿の仕上げ中に[[大坂の陣]]及び[[豊臣氏]]の滅亡があり、建物の内部が未完成のままとなっている。[[江戸幕府]]も200石の社領を安堵し、数度にわたり社殿の修復を行った。 神宮寺の長野山護国寺は塔頭十五坊を誇っていたが（誉田八幡宮は社人十三家）、[[明治]]初年の[[神仏分離]]、[[廃仏毀釈]]により本堂などの建物が取り壊され、南大門のみが残された。また、[[府社]]に列せられている。明治には幕末の剣客として知られる[[桃井春蔵|桃井直正]]が宮司を務めた。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、[[仲哀天皇]] == 境内 == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|南大門}}]] * 本殿 - [[慶長]]11年（[[1606年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]が[[片桐且元]]を普請奉行として再建。 * 中門 - 慶長11年（1606年）に豊臣秀頼が片桐且元を普請奉行として再建。 * 拝殿（割拝殿） - 慶長11年（1606年）に豊臣秀頼が片桐且元を普請奉行として再建。[[寛永]]年間（[[1624年]] - [[1645年]]）に改修。 * 当宗社（まさむねしゃ） - 祭神：[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]。[[式内社]]で、旧社格は無格社。元々は誉田八幡宮より東100mほど先にある当宗垣内という場所に鎮座していたが、[[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）11月16日に当社の境内へ遷座した[http://www.buccyake-kojiki.com/archives/1063947330.html 当宗神社 大阪府羽曳野市誉田 - 神社と古事記]2019年3月17日　閲覧。旧地一帯は5世紀頃、[[楽浪郡]]からの[[渡来人]]である当宗忌寸が居住するようになり、後に子孫にあたる当宗直が治めていた。[[宇多天皇]]の外祖母にあたる人物が当宗氏であったため、当宗忌寸の祖神である山陽公（『[[寛平御記]]』には河内当宗神とされている）が祀られるようになり、[[延喜式神名帳]]における「當宗神社三座」と比定されている。 * 放生橋（羽曳野市指定有形文化財） - 17世紀後半から18世紀前半頃に建造。かつてはこの橋を渡って[[誉田御廟山古墳]]の後円部の墳丘に参拝できた。 * 石造[[多宝塔]] - 石造の[[三重塔]]。 * 姫待[[稲荷社]] * [[えびす|恵比寿]]社 * 絵馬堂 * 安産社 * 南大門 - 旧長野山護国寺の南大門。 * 神庫 * 拝観庫 * 御輿庫 * 誉田宝殿 * 放生池 * 社務所 File:Konda-hachimangu, Masamune-jinja.jpg|当宗社（式内社） File:Konda-hachimangu, houjo-bashi.jpg|放生橋 File:Konda-hachimangu, soutou.jpg|層塔 File:Konda-hachimangu, sekihi.jpg|誉田林古戦場址碑 == 祭事 == === 日の丸神酒 === [[元日]]の[[歳旦祭]]をはじめ、正月三が日に無料授与される[[神酒]]が地元[[河内国|河内]]産の[[赤ワイン]]となっており、白い盃とのコントラストから「[[日本の国旗|日の丸]]神酒」と呼ばれている[http://travel-minakawa.com/2019/01/02/hinomarumiki/ 誉田八幡宮で「日の丸神酒」を頂いて来ました。- ミナカワ]2019年3月17日　閲覧。羽曳野市や[[柏原市]]は大阪府における[[ブドウ|ぶどう]]の主産地で、[[日本のワイン#主な生産地|ワイン]]の醸造も早くから行われている。 == 文化財 == === 国宝 === * 塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿（ちりじらでんこんどうそうみこし）（[[鎌倉時代]]） * 金銅透彫鞍金具 2具分 応神天皇陵陪塚丸山古墳出土 ** 附：金銅轡鏡板1、金銅花形辻金具一括､鹿角装刀残欠一括、馬具鉄鏃及び鐙等残闕一括 ** 附：発掘関係書類1 === 重要文化財 === * 紙本著色神功皇后縁起 2巻（[[室町時代]]） * 絹本著色誉田宗廟縁起 3巻（室町時代） * 舞楽面11面（二ノ舞2、散手1、貴徳1、天童1、陵王1、還城楽1、退走徳1、陵王1、納曽利2）附:退走徳4面（鎌倉 - 室町時代） * 松皮菱螺鈿鏡鞍（鏡地板共）附：杏葉轡（鎌倉時代） * 剣 銘真守（鎌倉時代） * 太刀 銘則国（鎌倉時代） * 薙刀 無銘大和物・鉄蛭巻薙刀拵（鎌倉時代） * 伏見天皇宸翰後撰和歌集 巻第廿（鎌倉時代） === 重要美術品 === * 鉄蛭巻薙刀拵 * [[後光厳天皇|後光厳院]]宸翰御消息 * 紺紙金地[[妙法蓮華経]] 1巻 * [[霊元天皇]]宸翰御消息 === 大阪府指定有形文化財 === * 太刀 銘高井信吉 附：梨子地糸巻太刀拵 === 大阪府指定天然記念物 === * 誉田八幡宮の[[エノキ]] === 羽曳野市指定有形文化財 === * 伝僧形八幡神像 1幅 （鎌倉時代） * 座太鼓 1基 [[応永]]17年（[[1410年]]）作。 * 羯鼓胴 1基 附：羯鼓台 1基 [[正平 (日本)|正平]]6年（[[1351年]]）に[[天王寺]]の怜人が製作。 * 石造燈籠 1基 [[寛政]]18年（[[1641年]]）大橋龍慶の寄進。 * 放生橋 === 羽曳野市有形民俗文化財 === * 藤花車 1台（江戸時代） * 藤花車飾幕 9点（江戸時代） == 前後の札所 == ; [[河内飛鳥古社寺霊場]] : 客番 [[住吉大社]]　-　'''客番 誉田八幡宮''' == 交通アクセス == * [[近鉄南大阪線]] [[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]、徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[誉田八幡神社]]（曖昧さ回避） * [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ 誉田八幡宮（公式サイト）] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html 誉田八幡宮 - 大阪府神社庁] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんたはちまんくう}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (工芸品)]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:大阪府内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形民俗文化財]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]] [[Category:欽明天皇]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[誉田八幡宮]] to [[Konda Hachimangu]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

神社と古事記]2019年3月17日　閲覧。旧地一帯は5世紀頃、[[楽浪郡]]からの[[渡来人]]である当宗忌寸が居住するようになり、後に子孫にあたる当宗直が治めていた。[[宇多天皇]]の外祖母にあたる人物が当宗氏であったため、当宗忌寸の祖神である山陽公（『[[寛平御記]]』には河内当宗神とされている）が祀られるようになり、[[延喜式神名帳]]における「當宗神社三座」と比定されている。 * 放生橋（羽曳野市指定有形文化財） - 17世紀後半から18世紀前半頃に建造。かつてはこの橋を渡って[[誉田御廟山古墳]]の後円部の墳丘に参拝できた。 * 石造[[多宝塔]] - 石造の[[三重塔]]。 * 姫待[[稲荷社]] * [[えびす|恵比寿]]社 * 絵馬堂 * 安産社 * 南大門 - 旧長野山護国寺の南大門。 * 神庫 * 拝観庫 * 御輿庫 * 誉田宝殿 * 放生池 * 社務所 File:Konda-hachimangu, Masamune-jinja.jpg|当宗社（式内社） File:Konda-hachimangu, houjo-bashi.jpg|放生橋 File:Konda-hachimangu, soutou.jpg|層塔 File:Konda-hachimangu, sekihi.jpg|誉田林古戦場址碑 == 祭事 == === 日の丸神酒 === [[元日]]の[[歳旦祭]]をはじめ、正月三が日に無料授与される[[神酒]]が地元[[河内国|河内]]産の[[赤ワイン]]となっており、白い盃とのコントラストから「[[日本の国旗|日の丸]]神酒」と呼ばれている[http://travel-minakawa.com/2019/01/02/hinomarumiki/ 誉田八幡宮で「日の丸神酒」を頂いて来ました。- ミナカワ]2019年3月17日　閲覧。羽曳野市や[[柏原市]]は大阪府における[[ブドウ|ぶどう]]の主産地で、[[日本のワイン#主な生産地|ワイン]]の醸造も早くから行われている。 == 文化財 == === 国宝 === * 塵地螺鈿金銅装神輿（ちりじらでんこんどうそうみこし）（[[鎌倉時代]]） * 金銅透彫鞍金具 2具分 応神天皇陵陪塚丸山古墳出土 ** 附：金銅轡鏡板1、金銅花形辻金具一括､鹿角装刀残欠一括、馬具鉄鏃及び鐙等残闕一括 ** 附：発掘関係書類1 === 重要文化財 === * 紙本著色神功皇后縁起 2巻（[[室町時代]]） * 絹本著色誉田宗廟縁起 3巻（室町時代） * 舞楽面11面（二ノ舞2、散手1、貴徳1、天童1、陵王1、還城楽1、退走徳1、陵王1、納曽利2）附:退走徳4面（鎌倉 - 室町時代） * 松皮菱螺鈿鏡鞍（鏡地板共）附：杏葉轡（鎌倉時代） * 剣 銘真守（鎌倉時代） * 太刀 銘則国（鎌倉時代） * 薙刀 無銘大和物・鉄蛭巻薙刀拵（鎌倉時代） * 伏見天皇宸翰後撰和歌集 巻第廿（鎌倉時代） === 重要美術品 === * 鉄蛭巻薙刀拵 * [[後光厳天皇|後光厳院]]宸翰御消息 * 紺紙金地[[妙法蓮華経]] 1巻 * [[霊元天皇]]宸翰御消息 === 大阪府指定有形文化財 === * 太刀 銘高井信吉 附：梨子地糸巻太刀拵 === 大阪府指定天然記念物 === * 誉田八幡宮の[[エノキ]] === 羽曳野市指定有形文化財 === * 伝僧形八幡神像 1幅 （鎌倉時代） * 座太鼓 1基 [[応永]]17年（[[1410年]]）作。 * 羯鼓胴 1基 附：羯鼓台 1基 [[正平 (日本)|正平]]6年（[[1351年]]）に[[天王寺]]の怜人が製作。 * 石造燈籠 1基 [[寛政]]18年（[[1641年]]）大橋龍慶の寄進。 * 放生橋 === 羽曳野市有形民俗文化財 === * 藤花車 1台（江戸時代） * 藤花車飾幕 9点（江戸時代） == 前後の札所 == ; [[河内飛鳥古社寺霊場]] : 客番 [[住吉大社]]　-　'''客番 誉田八幡宮''' == 交通アクセス == * [[近鉄南大阪線]] [[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]、徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[誉田八幡神社]]（曖昧さ回避） * [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ 誉田八幡宮（公式サイト）] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/habikino-city/11002kondahachimangu.html 誉田八幡宮 - 大阪府神社庁] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんたはちまんくう}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (工芸品)]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:大阪府内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形民俗文化財]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]] [[Category:欽明天皇]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|12=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|12=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}} * {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|12=draft}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|12=draft}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|12=draft}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|12=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|12=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|12=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|12=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|12=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|12=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|12=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|12=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|12=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}} *
