{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|qq=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|qq=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|qq=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|qq=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|qq=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Konda Hachimangu}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Archaeological Materials)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Fusha Ranked Shrines]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Historical Artifacts)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Temples and Shrines Dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] [[sn:Konda Hachimangu]] [[sn:onda Hachimangu]] [[en:Konda Hachimangu]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|qq=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|qq=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|qq=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|qq=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|qq=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Archaeological Materials)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Fusha Ranked Shrines]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Historical Artifacts)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Temples and Shrines Dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] }} The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|qq=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|qq=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|qq=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|qq=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|qq=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Archaeological Materials)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Historical Artifacts)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Temples and Shrines Dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Konda Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|qq=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|qq=draft}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|qq=draft}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|qq=draft}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|qq=draft}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|qq=draft}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|qq=draft}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|qq=}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}} Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} | Established = 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|qq=}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|qq=}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern typeing System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana|qq=}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Reizei|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto clan|qq=}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshinori|qq=}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanboku-chō period|qq=}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagiri Katsumoto|qq=}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|qq=}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|qq=}} | established = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|qq=}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern typeing System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana|qq=}}. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}} established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Reizei|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}} became the guardian deity of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto clan|qq=}}, shrine restoration was carried out by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|ja|足利義教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshinori|qq=}} donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanboku-chō period|qq=}} to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda Nobunaga|qq=}}, the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|ja|片桐且元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katagiri Katsumoto|qq=}} as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the {{ill|Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂の陣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Osaka|qq=}}. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} ** {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} ** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from {{ill|Furuichi Station (Osaka)|ja|古市駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Archaeological Materials)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Historical Artifacts)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] [[sn:Konda Hachimangu]] [[sn:onda Hachimangu]] [[en:Konda Hachimangu]]

The shrine's former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}.
