{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] | Established = 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|qq=}} | established = [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|qq=}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Archaeological Materials)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Historical Artifacts)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] [[sn:Konda Hachimangu]] [[sn:onda Hachimangu]] [[en:Konda Hachimangu]] The shrine's former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Archaeological Materials)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Historical Artifacts)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] [[sn:Konda Hachimangu]] [[sn:onda Hachimangu]] [[en:Konda Hachimangu]] However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] | Established = 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|qq=}} | established = [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|qq=}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] | Established = 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|qq=}} | established = [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|qq=}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Archaeological Materials)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Kinmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] [[sn:Konda Hachimangu]] [[sn:onda Hachimangu]] [[en:Konda Hachimangu]] {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Konda Hachimangu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] | Established = 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|qq=}} | established = [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|qq=}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Archaeological Materials)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Historical Artifacts)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] }} [[ja:誉田八幡宮]] [[sn:Konda Hachimangu]] [[sn:onda Hachimangu]] [[en:Konda Hachimangu]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形民俗文化財]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:欽明天皇]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

The shrine's former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kondahachimangu.com/ Official Website] * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/daiddshibu/habikino-city/dd002kondahachimangu.html Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association: Konda Hachimangū] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|誉田八幡宮|version=102240604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura period) * Gold-copper fittings excavated from the Maruyama Kofun (accessory items related to Emperor Ōjin's tomb) === Important Cultural Properties === * Illustrated scrolls related to Empress Jingū (Muromachi period) * Scrolls depicting the Konda Mausoleum (Muromachi period) * Various ceremonial dance masks (Kamakura–Muromachi periods) * Mirror saddle with decorative metal fittings (Kamakura period) === Other Cultural Designations === * Various Osaka Prefecture and Habikino City cultural properties == Access == * **By train:** Approximately 10 minutes’ walk from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Konda Hachimangu|ja|誉田八幡神社 Haiden (worship hall) | location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] Konda 3-chome 2-8 | Latitude = 34.558861 | Longitude = 135.609889 | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka | Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] | Established = 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559) | Main Building = | Other names = | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Ancient Shrines and Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内飛鳥古寺霊場}} (guest shrine) | festival = September 15 | Rituals = | name = Konda Hachiman Shrine | image = [[File:Konda-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Main hall (Haiden) | location = [[Habikino]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34 | lat_min = 33 | lat_sec = 31.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | longitude = 135 | long_min = 36 | long_sec = 35.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Osaka | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] | Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|ja|府社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha (prefectural-Ranked shrines)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | established = [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] 20th year (559 CE) | hon-den = | alternate_names = | pilgrimage_sites = {{ill|Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|ja|河内飛鳥古社寺霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Asuka Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 河内飛鳥古寺霊場}} (Guest station) | festival = September 15 }} [[File:Konda-hachimangu, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Konda Hachimangū''' (誉田八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine (府社). It is situated adjacent to the southern part of the {{ill|Kondoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, the mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established in the 20th year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (559 CE) by imperial decree aimed at restoring the kingdom of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. The shrine was initially a mausoleum established in front of Emperor Ōjin’s tomb and is considered the oldest Hachiman shrine.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} However an alternative theory says [[Kaijin Shrine]] is the oldest [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].{{Cite book|last=Rambelli|first=F|title=The Sea and The Sacred in Japan|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic Publishing|year=2018|isbn=978-1350062870|location=Camden|pages=preface}}{{Rp|page=97–99}} This location is historically significant to Emperor Ōjin. He spent his early years in this area, and his consort, {{ill|Nakatsuhime|ja|仲津姫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was the daughter of Konda no Mawakao (誉田真若王), the regional ruler of this land.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} In the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] established the associated temple of Naganozan Gokokuji (長野山護国寺) within the shrine grounds. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], in 1051 (Eishō 6), the shrine was relocated about 1 chō (approximately 109 meters) south of its original location to its current site. This relocation coincided with a royal visit by [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]].{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} Since [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] became the guardian deity of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]], shrine restoration was carried out by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] in dd96 (Kenkyū 7), with the dedication of a portable shrine (神輿) and other treasures designated as national treasures. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshinori|Ashikaga Yoshinori]] donated the Important Cultural Properties "Konda Sōbyō Engi" and "Jingū Kōgō Engi." The shrine received patronage from successive generations of samurai, contributing to its prosperity. From the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it was protected by the Konda Mikawa Nyūdō family, who held the position of shrine administrator. However, during internal conflicts among the Hatakeyama clan, the shrine buildings and temple halls were burned down and fell into disrepair. During the time of [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]], the shrine's lands were confiscated. Later, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] donated 200 koku of land and rebuilt the shrine buildings. However, the shrine was destroyed by fire again in 1586 (Tenshō 14). The land allocation was later shifted to Komuro Village in Shiki District. In 1606 (Keichō dd), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] appointed [[:en:Katagiri Katsumoto|Katagiri Katsumoto]] as the construction supervisor to rebuild the main hall, middlegate, and worship hall. However, the internal construction remained unfinished due to the fall of the Toyotomi clan following the [[:en:Siege of Osaka|Siege of Osaka]]. The shrine lands were maintained under the Tokugawa shogunate, which also carried out periodic repairs. The temple associated with the shrine, Naganozan Gokokuji, once included 15 sub-temples, while the shrine itself had 13 shrine families. However, with the Meiji Restoration’s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] and [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] policies, many temple buildings were dismantled, leaving only the South Gate.{{Sfn|Habikino City|2019}} The shrine was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine (府社) under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * **Primary Deities** ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] ** [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] == Grounds == [[File:Konda-hachimangu, minamimon.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|South Gate}}]] * **Main Hall** - Reconstructed in 1606 (Keichō dd) under Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto. * **Middle Gate** - Also rebuilt in 1606. * **Worship Hall (割拝殿, Waribai-den)** - Built in 1606 and renovated during the Kan’ei era (1624–1645). * **Masamune Shrine (当宗社)** - Enshrines {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. Originally located about 100 meters east of its current location. The area was historically inhabited by descendants of Korean immigrants.{{Sfn|神社と古事記|2019}} * **Hōjō Bridge** - Built in the late 17th to early 18th century and designated a Tangible Cultural Property by Habikino City. * **Stone Pagoda** - Three-tiered stone pagoda. * **Himemachi Inari Shrine** * **Ebisu Shrine** * **Ema Hall** * **Anzan Shrine** * **Treasury and Other Facilities:** ** South Gate ** Shrine Storehouse ** Sacred Object Storehouse ** Shrine Administration Office == Festivals == === Hinomaru Sacred Wine (日の丸神酒) === During the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=New Year’s Festival|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} and the first three days of January, sacred wine is distributed free of charge. Locally produced red wine is used, and its contrast against the white cup has earned it the name "Hinomaru Sacred Wine," referring to Japan’s national flag.{{Sfn|Minakawa|2019}} Habikino and neighboring Kashiwara City are known for their grape production and early wine brewing traditions. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasures === * Lacquered and inlaid portable shrine (Kamakura
